    Find A Grant
    Global Search Dialog

      Powering Leadership to Transform Health

      Investing in leaders who are changing policies and practices in healthcare and other systems so everyone has a fair chance at a healthy life.

      Your health shouldn’t be determined by who you are, your background or your ZIP code. But the health disparities created by structural racism shorten lifespans and lower quality of life. Leadership is a critical tool to remove these obstacles to wellbeing and ensure everyone can access what they need to be healthy.

       

      About

      Impact | Initiatives | Resources

       

      RWJF invests in health and social justice leaders breaking down barriers to health equity in the U.S. by changing the racist or unfair policies, systems, and environments that create them.

      We champion established leaders who are successfully advancing change and help emerging changemakers develop leadership skills to transform health. We also support programs and initiatives that build networks of leaders to reimagine systems, institutions, and communities.

      We prioritize investment in three areas:

      We equip committed leaders to dismantle structural racism in the healthcare system

       

      We support the leadership of Black women, Indigenous women, and women of color who are advancing economic justice

       

      We accelerate leaders’ efforts to make health systems equitable and representative of the people they serve

       

      Transforming Systems, Policies, and Practices

      Across communities and networks, supported leaders are challenging structural racism in healthcare, transforming health systems to better reflect the communities they serve and designing more equitable policies, practices and environments.

      Using bold approaches and remaining accountable to communities harmed by unjust systems, these changemakers are reshaping who has power, how decisions are made and what an equitable future for all looks like.

      Featured Initiatives

      These are a few of the leadership initiatives driving us closer to health equity:

      Black Women’s Executive Directors Program

      Supports Black-women-led social and racial justice organizations by expanding their directors’ leadership skills; providing wellness resources for growth, healing and collective power; and supporting operations. 

      Health Leadership Development Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion
      Learn more about the program

      Community Solutions for Health Equity

      A growing collaborative partnership between community-based organizations across the U.S. that centers cultural knowledge and community priorities as solutions to mitigate health care systems barriers.

      Learn more about CSHE

      Consortium Dedicated to Health Equity Education

      Provides support and community to students, faculty, educators and administrators in the Greater Philadelphia area to advance equity in healthcare education. 

      Learn more about the consortium

      HBCU Medical School Initiative

      In partnership with medical schools at HBCUs, equips leaders to transform health systems, build a diverse health workforce, and provide culturally competent care, research and tools that address health disparities.

      Health Leadership Development Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion
      Learn more about the initiative

      JUSTICE SQUARED

      Builds skills and relationships for teams of leaders from community organizations and healthcare institutions. Participants work together to tackle real-world challenges rooted in structural racism.

      Learn more about JUSTICE SQUARED

      Additional Programs

      Health Policy Research Scholars (HPRS)

      Develops the leadership skills of doctoral students from diverse fields and historically marginalized backgrounds so they can inform and influence policies that advance health equity and move the U.S. toward a society where health is a shared value and every person can reach their best health.

      Learn more about HPRS

       

      Previous Initiatives

      If you have questions about a previous RWJF leadership development program, please contact us at .

       

      RWJF Topic

      Leadership to Impact Health

      We work to prepare nationwide communities for leadership roles in policy and practice in order to create healthier places to live, learn, work, and play.

      Learn more

      Resources for Leaders

      Access tools and resources to support your work dismantling structural racism and creating conditions to make health systems and professions more diverse, equitable and inclusive.

      Communications Planning Tool

      The tool is designed to help you create and implement a proactive plan. It will help you engage more intentionally and strategically in times of urgency and changing circumstances. And it should complement other communication planning tools and training you may have received. If you already have a communications plan, reviewing this tool might help you add new insights and dimensions to it.

      Tool PDF, English
      Tool PDF, Spanish
      Video, English
      Video, Spanish

      Stay informed

      Sign up for the latest funding alerts, news, research, and perspectives from RWJF.

      Email address already subscribed

      Email address already subscribed. Please check your inbox to manage your subscriptions.

      Subscribed!

      Thank you. You are now subscribed.

      Are you an RWJF Grantee (Past or Present?)
      Are you a European Union Resident?
      Tell us what type of content you want to receive. Select at least one (required).

      Be informed with our twice a month newsletter updating you with relevant news and research around a Culture of Health, as well as the latest funding opportunities.

      Get funded by RWJF: Receive notifications when new funding opportunities are released.

      Receive monthly updates on RWJF-sponsored research that informs many robust health policy debates on Capitol Hill, covering topics like health equity, improving access to quality healthcare, equitable housing, and more.

      Shop talk for researchers. This monthly newsletter covers research news and opportunities from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation.

      Communications expedite action. Get periodic research and expert insights on the best ways to communicate so we can spread what works.

      Sometimes we have news, announcements or opportunities that don't quite fit the subscription parameters above. If you're interested, we'll send you this information under "There's more...". *If you've indicated you are an EU resident, we will only send these communications if you intentionally check this box.

      Which profession or pursuit best describes you?
